task_completion.description: Verify the creation of a bone segmentation mask using the specified Hounsfield Unit threshold.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the segmentation panel in the medical imaging software (e.g., InVesalius). The threshold values should be explicitly set to Min: 226 and Max: 3071 HU, and a colored mask overlay should appear on the bone structures in the 2D views.

task_completion.description: Verify the agent navigates to the bottom axial slices to locate the cervical vertebrae.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the axial view panel and a slice slider or index indicator showing the current slice is between 1 and 10 (out of 108).

task_completion.description: Verify the mask content is removed from axial slices 1 through 10.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the agent using a manual mask editing or eraser tool in the axial view on slices 1-10. The colored mask overlay should be completely removed from the bone structures in these specific bottom slices.

task_completion.description: Verify the rest of the skull remains segmented.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for screenshots showing higher axial slices (e.g., slice 11 to 108, or a 3D surface view of the skull). The colored mask overlay must still be visible on the skull structures, confirming they were not accidentally erased.

task_completion.description: Verify the modified mask is exported as a NIfTI file to the correct path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for an 'Export Mask' or 'Save As' dialog box. The file name must be 'cleaned_skull.nii.gz' and the directory path must be '/home/ga/Documents/'.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the medical imaging software's graphical interface to perform the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: Check that the agent interacted with the GUI (e.g., InVesalius) to threshold and edit the mask, rather than writing a Python script or using terminal commands (like fslmaths) to bypass the manual editing process.

integrity.description: Verify the agent actually used the software's manual editing/erasing tools on the mask.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for the selection and use of brush/eraser tools in the GUI, ensuring the agent didn't just crop the entire image volume or create a blank file.

integrity.description: Verify the export was done through the software's export dialog.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for the software's native save/export dialog being used to generate the .nii.gz file, rather than terminal commands touching or echoing a file into existence.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The dataset has 108 slices. The bone segmentation threshold must be exactly 226 to 3071 HU. The bottom 10 axial slices (slices 1-10) must have their mask completely cleared. The rest of the skull (e.g., center slice 54, which contains >17,000 bone voxels) must remain segmented. The final mask must be exported as a NIfTI file to /home/ga/Documents/cleaned_skull.nii.gz.
